summary: 2026-07-02 09:09:51 PDT — The branch satisfies the CHANGELOG file-presence gate, deletes this repo's CHANGELOG.md, keeps BACKLOG.md/workflow-protocol.md coherent, and preserves the AUDIT- anchor. I found one remaining wording issue in /close's zero-record path: later merge steps still name the merge candidate as a record commit/head even when step 5(b) explicitly makes no record commit. reviewer_test.sh and dev_audit_test.sh passed; guard_test.sh could not be rerun in this read-only sandbox because its temp repo setup was denied, so that is not counted as a diff finding.

findings.title: Zero-record path still assumes a record commit in later step wording
findings.claim: Step 5(b) now correctly says to make no record commit when there is no CHANGELOG.md and no tracked backlog item, but step 5(c) still says "Re-gate the record commit" and step 5(d)'s nearby wording calls the merge target the "gated record HEAD" / "approved fixes + records". In the zero-record case that commit/head does not contain release records, so the procedure remains internally inconsistent on the exact edge case AC1 called out.
findings.suggestion: Rename 5(c)/(d) around the merge candidate instead of the record commit, e.g. "Re-gate HEAD" and "Merge the gated HEAD", and change the remote comments to "approved fixes + any release records".
